agents.name: Planner
agents.lastMessage: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity wi

report.hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 residues (e.g., 192/193/205), while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will disrupt off-target SSTR3/5 interactions via electrostatic repulsion, thereby increasing selectivity margin.

agents.name: Critic
agents.lastMessage: The current design strategy is not generating sufficient diversity in non-pharma

report.hypothesis: The current design strategy is not generating sufficient diversity in non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt off-target binding. We must explicitly target these positions with mutations that reduce SSTR3/SSTR5 interactions.

proposed_changes.rationale: To increase selectivity, we must disrupt conserved contacts with off-targets SSTR3/SSTR5. These positions are non-pharmacophore and can be mutated to reduce off-target binding.
These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining or improving SSTR2 binding. They are based on known differences in receptor pocket geometries.
